workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you extract the deployment blockers from this email thread for Lucerne Publishing? We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 10:48 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with deployment blockers. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 15:57 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 11:55 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with deployment blockers. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-B trap (Under-escalates serious issue); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-B failure mode (Under-escalates serious issue)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-B -- Under-escalates serious issue
